(2) the specific language about Class B holders electing 51% of directors; (3) any statement that Musk will beneficially own a majority of Class B stock and majority of voting power; (4) the exact wording on the cover page about shares outstanding after the offering; (5) any disclosure about the conversion of Class C shares to Class A before the offering.

tool_output.output:
Based on the provided text, here is the exact language addressing each of your five inquiries:

**(1) Whether Mr. Musk will have majority voting control after the IPO - definitive fact or blank percentages?**
The text uses both definitive statements and blank percentages depending on the section:
*   **Definitive Fact:** "Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (the Class A common stock and the Class B common stock voting together) and therefore will be able to elect all the members of our board."
*   **Definitive Fact with Blank Shares:** "Because Mr. Musk will beneficially own [blank] shares of Class A common stock and [blank] shares of Class B common stock, which represents greater than 50% of the voting power of our common stock with respect to director elections..."
*   **Blank Percentages:** "Assuming an offering size as set forth above and an initial public offering price of $ [blank] per share... Elon Musk... will hold approximately [blank] % of the voting power of our common stock (or approximately [blank] % if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full) immediately after the completion of this offering..."

**(2) The specific language about Class B holders electing 51% of directors**
The text states: 
*   "...holders of our Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, are entitled to elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors (rounded up to the nearest whole number)..."
*   "...holders of our Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, will be entitled to elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors constituting our board (rounded up to the nearest whole number)..."

**(3) Statement that Musk will beneficially own a majority of Class B stock and majority of voting power**
The exact quote is: "Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (the Class A common stock and the Class B common stock voting together) and therefore will be able to elect all the members of our board."

**(4) Exact wording on the cover page about shares outstanding after the offering**
The cover page summary table states:
*   "| Class A common stock outstanding immediately after this offering ................................................ | shares (or shares if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full). |"
*   "| Class B common stock outstanding immediately after this offering ................................................ | shares. |"

**(5) Any disclosure about the conversion of Class C shares to Class A before the offering**
There is **no disclosure** in the provided text regarding the conversion of Class C shares to Class A. The text only mentions Class C shares to state that they will have no voting rights and that no shares will be issued or outstanding: *"10,000,000,000 shares of Class C common stock, par value $0.001 per share, of which no shares will be issued and outstanding"* and *"the holders of our Class C common stock will have no voting rights."*
